title: Azalea hypoglauca
intro: Azalea hypoglauca, also known as the 'Asian Rhododendron', is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. Traditionally, Azalea hypoglauca has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Azalea hypoglauca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional East Asian medicine, Azalea hypoglauca is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Azalea hypoglauca is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). It is native to parts of Asia. The classification of Azalea hypoglauca can be broken down as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Ericales, Family: Ericaceae, Genus: Rhododendron, Species: R. hypoglauca (though some sources consider it a synonym of Rhododendron glauca). This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

preparations: Azalea hypoglauca is primarily used in traditional medicine in China, where it is valued for its purported health benefits.  The plant's herbal preparations are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
